attributes.action: Make First Aid Education Mandatory in all UK schools.
attributes.additional_details: Young people face emergencies but many don’t know how to respond. 532,000 teenagers helped an injured or unwell friend last year, yet nearly half panicked or didn’t know what to do. Around 400,000 students are injured at school annually, not including adults. First aid training saves lives, builds confidence, and creates safer school communities.
attributes.background: Introduce compulsory first aid education in all UK schools, including CPR, basic life support, and emergency response skills. Add first aid to the national curriculum so every student receives essential, age-appropriate training.

rejection.details:
We are not clear from your petition how you would like the current practice in schools to change. Information about the current practice is available here: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/relationships-education-relationships-and-sex-education-rse-and-health-education/physical-health-and-mental-wellbeing-primary-and-secondary#by-the-end-of-primary-school

You can start a new petition setting out more clearly how you would like the current practice to change.
